testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.title: Auto-trigger learnings on epic completion & refactor epic header actions
acceptanceCriteria: Learnings auto-trigger when last ticket in epic completes
Manual "Refresh Learnings" button exists in learnings section
Re-running learnings works correctly (updates, not duplicates)
Epic detail header actions are cleaner and less crowded
userStories.description:
Two tasks:

## 1. Auto-Trigger Learnings on Epic Completion

When all tickets in an epic move to "done", automatically trigger the learnings reconciliation process. This eliminates the "No learnings captured yet" message that currently shows on completed epics.

**Behavior:**
- When a ticket status changes to "done", check if ALL tickets in its epic are now done
- If yes, automatically run the learnings extraction/reconciliation for that epic
- Add a manual "Refresh Learnings" button on the epic detail page for re-running when new tickets are added or learnings need updating
- The manual button should be visible in the learnings section (not the header)

## 2. Refactor Epic Detail Header Actions

The header action buttons (Ship Changes, Review Ticket, Findings, Edit, Launch) are getting crowded on a single row. Refactor into a cleaner layout:
- Keep primary action (Ship Changes) prominent
- Group secondary actions (Review, Findings, Edit) into a dropdown or overflow menu  
- Keep Launch dropdown as-is

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Learnings auto-trigger when last ticket in epic completes
- [ ] Manual "Refresh Learnings" button exists in learnings section
- [ ] Re-running learnings works correctly (updates, not duplicates)
- [ ] Epic detail header actions are cleaner and less crowded
- [ ] All existing button functionality preserved
- [ ] Responsive at narrower widths

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicDescription:
# Plan: Cost-Per-Ticket Tracking (Real Token Data)

## Context

Brain Dump has telemetry infrastructure (sessions, events, tool call tracking) and a dashboard with charts, but **token usage is never captured** — the `totalTokens` field is always NULL because Claude Code doesn't expose token counts to MCP servers. The dashboard's Token Usage chart permanently shows "No token usage data yet."

The self-telemetry in `mcp-server/lib/telemetry-self-log.ts:322` calls `endTelemetrySession` with just `{ sessionId, outcome }` — no token data. The schema and UI exist for tokens but nothing flows in.

**Solution**: Parse Claude Code's JSONL transcript files (which contain full `usage` data per API response) via a Stop hook, and provide a universal MCP intake API for other providers. Real data only, no estimation.

**Constraint**: No new MCP tools — only new actions on existing `telemetry` and `admin` tools.

---

## Approach: Real Token Data via Three Collection Layers

**Layer 1 — Stop Hook (Claude Code)**: An 11th hook fires at session end, receives `transcript_path`, parses the JSONL file for actual token counts per model (input, output, cache_read, cache_creation), and records them via Brain Dump CLI.

**Layer 2 — OpenTelemetry Ingestion** (Phase 2, documented but not built in v1): A lightweight OTEL collector receives `claude_code.token.usage` and `claude_code.cost.usage` metrics. Provider-agnostic — any tool that exports OTEL can feed data in.

**Layer 3 — MCP Intake API**: The `telemetry` tool accepts `record-usage` actions from any environment. Cursor, VS Code, OpenCode, or custom tools can self-report token usage directly. Universal fallback.

If no real data exists for a session, the UI shows nothing (no estimates).

---

## Data Collection Details

### Stop Hook (`capture-token-usage.sh`)

1. Receives `transcript_path` from Claude Code Stop event (via stdin JSON)
2. Calls `scripts/parse-transcript-tokens.ts` which parses the JSONL
3. JSONL format: each line is a JSON object, assistant messages include `usage`:
   ```json
   {"type":"assistant","message":{"usage":{"input_tokens":12500,"output_tokens":3200,"cache_read_input_tokens":45000,"cache_creation_input_tokens":8000},"model":"claude-opus-4-6-20250616"}}
   ```
4. Groups by model, sums all token types
5. Detects active Brain Dump session from `.claude/ralph-state.json` (or finds by timestamp)
6. Calls `brain-dump telemetry record-usage --session <id> --model <name> --input <n> --output <n> --cache-read <n> --cache-create <n>`
7. Installed via existing `scripts/setup-claude-code.sh`

### OTEL Collector (Phase 2 — document only)

- Config: `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=1` + `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:4318`
- Brain Dump would run a minimal OTLP HTTP receiver that writes to SQLite
- Maps `session.id` from OTEL spans to Brain Dump telemetry session IDs

---

## Schema Changes

### New table: `cost_models`

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| id | TEXT PK | |
| provider | TEXT NOT NULL | 'anthropic', 'openai', 'google', etc. |
| model_name | TEXT NOT NULL | 'claude-opus-4-6', 'gpt-4o', etc. |
| input_cost_per_mtok | REAL NOT NULL | USD per 1M input tokens |
| output_cost_per_mtok | REAL NOT NULL | USD per 1M output tokens |
| cache_read_cost_per_mtok | REAL | USD per 1M cache read tokens |
| cache_create_cost_per_mtok | REAL | USD per 1M cache creation tokens |
| is_default | INTEGER | One model is default |
| created_at, updated_at | TEXT | |

### New table: `token_usage`

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| id | TEXT PK | |
| telemetry_session_id | TEXT FK | Links to telemetry_sessions |
| ticket_id | TEXT FK | Denormalized for fast queries |
| model | TEXT NOT NULL | Model used |
| input_tokens | INTEGER NOT NULL | |
| output_tokens | INTEGER NOT NULL | |
| cache_read_tokens | INTEGER | |
| cache_creation_tokens | INTEGER | |
| cost_usd | REAL | Computed from cost_models at insert time |
| source | TEXT NOT NULL | 'jsonl-hook', 'otel', 'mcp-manual' |
| recorded_at | TEXT NOT NULL | |

**Indexes**: telemetry_session_id, ticket_id, recorded_at, (ticket_id + recorded_at)

### Altered columns on `telemetry_sessions`

- `total_input_tokens` INTEGER (aggregated from token_usage)
- `total_output_tokens` INTEGER (aggregated from token_usage)
- `total_cost_usd` REAL (aggregated from token_usage)

---

## Core Logic

### New file: `core/cost.ts`

```typescript
// Recording
export function recordUsage(db: DbHandle, params: RecordUsageParams): TokenUsageRecord;
  // → inserts token_usage row
  // → looks up cost_models for the model, computes cost_usd
  // → updates telemetry_sessions aggregates (total_input_tokens, total_output_tokens, total_cost_usd)

// Queries
export function getTicketCost(db: DbHandle, ticketId: string): TicketCostResult;
  // → aggregates token_usage rows for all sessions linked to this ticket

export function getEpicCost(db: DbHandle, epicId: string): EpicCostResult;
  // → aggregates across all tickets in the epic

export function getProjectCost(db: DbHandle, projectId: string): ProjectCostResult;
  // → aggregates across all tickets in the project

export function getCostTrend(db: DbHandle, params: CostTrendParams): CostTrendResult;
  // → daily/weekly cost grouped by recorded_at date
  // → params: projectId?, epicId?, since?, granularity ('daily'|'weekly')

// Pricing CRUD
export function upsertCostModel(db: DbHandle, params: UpsertCostModelParams): CostModel;
export function listCostModels(db: DbHandle): CostModel[];
export function deleteCostModel(db: DbHandle, id: string): void;

// Internal
export function computeCostFromTokens(db: DbHandle, model: string, tokens: TokenCounts): number;
  // → looks up cost_models by model_name (fuzzy match), computes USD
  // → returns 0 if no matching model found (unknown model, not an error)
```

### New file: `cli/commands/telemetry-record-usage.ts`

CLI command called by the Stop hook:
```
brain-dump telemetry record-usage \
  --session <telemetry_session_id> \
  --model <model_name> \
  --input <input_tokens> \
  --output <output_tokens> \
  --cache-read <cache_read_tokens> \
  --cache-create <cache_creation_tokens> \
  --source jsonl-hook
```

Falls back to finding the most recent active telemetry session if `--session` not provided.

### New file: `scripts/parse-transcript-tokens.ts`

TypeScript helper called by the Stop hook:
```typescript
// Reads a Claude Code JSONL transcript file
// Parses each line, extracts usage objects from assistant messages
// Groups by model, sums all token types
// Outputs JSON: [{model, inputTokens, outputTokens, cacheReadTokens, cacheCreationTokens}]
```

### Modify: `core/index.ts`

Export the new `cost` module.

---

## Stop Hook Implementation

### New file: `.claude/hooks/capture-token-usage.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Event: Stop
# Parses Claude Code transcript JSONL for real token usage and records to Brain Dump

# 1. Read stdin JSON for transcript_path
# 2. Call parse-transcript-tokens.ts to extract token data
# 3. Detect active session from .claude/ralph-state.json
# 4. For each model in output, call: brain-dump telemetry record-usage ...
```

### Modify: `scripts/setup-claude-code.sh`

Add the new hook to the installation script so it gets registered in `~/.claude/hooks/`.

---

## MCP Changes (actions on existing tools only — NO new tools)

### Extend `telemetry` tool (mcp-server/tools/telemetry.ts)

3 new actions:

- **`record-usage`** — Universal intake. Any provider reports actual token counts.
  - Params: `sessionId?`, `ticketId?`, `model`, `inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `cacheReadTokens?`, `cacheCreationTokens?`, `source?`
  - Auto-detects session if not provided (same as existing telemetry tools)

- **`get-cost`** — Cost for a session, ticket, or epic.
  - Params: `sessionId?`, `ticketId?`, `epicId?`
  - Returns: total cost, token breakdown by model, per-session breakdown

- **`cost-trend`** — Cost over time for a project.
  - Params: `projectId`, `since?`, `granularity?` ('daily'|'weekly')
  - Returns: array of {period, totalCostUsd, tokensByModel}

### Extend `admin` tool (mcp-server/tools/admin.ts)

2 new actions:

- **`set-cost-model`** — Configure pricing for a provider/model.
  - Params: `provider`, `modelName`, `inputCostPerMtok`, `outputCostPerMtok`, `cacheReadCostPerMtok?`, `cacheCreateCostPerMtok?`

- **`list-cost-models`** — List all configured pricing models.

---

## UI Changes

### New dashboard components (src/components/dashboard/)

- **`CostPerTicketChart.tsx`** — Bar chart showing cost per completed ticket (last 30 days). Uses existing Recharts BarChart pattern from CommitsPerDayCard.tsx.

- **`CostTrendChart.tsx`** — Line chart showing daily/weekly cost trend. Uses existing Recharts LineChart pattern from VelocityChart.tsx.

- **`CostByEpicChart.tsx`** — Horizontal bar chart showing total cost per epic. Uses existing Recharts BarChart horizontal layout pattern from ToolCallDistributionChart.

### Replace: `TokenUsageOverTimeChart` (AITelemetryTab.tsx:423-476)

Replace the always-empty token chart with `CostTrendChart` showing actual cost data from `token_usage` table.

### New settings component

- **`CostModelSettings.tsx`** — Section in settings UI for managing pricing per provider/model. Table view with edit/add/delete. Seeded models shown as read-only with override option.

### New API: `src/api/cost.ts`

```typescript
export const getCostAnalytics = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
  .handler(async (): Promise<DashboardCostAnalytics>);

export const getTicketCost = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
  .inputValidator(...)
  .handler(async ({ data: ticketId }): Promise<TicketCostResult>);

export const getCostModels = createServerFn({ method: "GET" })
  .handler(async (): Promise<CostModel[]>);

export const updateCostModel = createServerFn({ method: "POST" })
  .inputValidator(...)
  .handler(async ({ data }): Promise<CostModel>);
```

### New hooks: `src/lib/hooks/cost.ts`

- `useCostAnalytics()` — dashboard-level cost data
- `useTicketCost(ticketId)` — per-ticket cost breakdown
- `useCostModels()` — pricing configuration
- `useUpdateCostModel()` — mutation for updating pricing

---

## Migration

1. **Schema migration**: `pnpm db:generate` + `pnpm db:migrate` for new tables and columns
2. **Seed cost models**: Insert current pricing for major providers:
   - Anthropic: Opus 4 ($15/$75), Sonnet 4 ($3/$15), Haiku 4.5 ($1/$5) per 1M tokens
   - OpenAI: GPT-4o ($2.50/$10), o3 ($10/$40)
   - Google: Gemini 2.5 Pro ($1.25/$10)
3. **Backfill** (optional, best-effort): Scan `~/.claude/projects/` for existing JSONL transcript files, parse tokens, match to telemetry sessions by timestamp overlap

---

## Critical Files

### Modified Files

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `src/lib/schema.ts` | New tables: cost_models, token_usage. New columns on telemetry_sessions |
| `core/index.ts` | Export cost module |
| `mcp-server/tools/telemetry.ts` | Add record-usage, get-cost, cost-trend actions |
| `mcp-server/tools/admin.ts` | Add set-cost-model, list-cost-models actions |
| `src/api/telemetry.ts` | Replace token chart query with cost data query |
| `src/components/dashboard/AITelemetryTab.tsx` | Replace TokenUsageOverTimeChart with CostTrendChart, add CostPerTicketChart, CostByEpicChart |
| `scripts/setup-claude-code.sh` | Register capture-token-usage.sh hook |

### New Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `core/cost.ts` | Token usage recording, cost computation, trend queries |
| `cli/commands/telemetry-record-usage.ts` | CLI command called by Stop hook |
| `scripts/parse-transcript-tokens.ts` | Parse Claude Code JSONL, sum tokens per model |
| `.claude/hooks/capture-token-usage.sh` | Stop hook: parse transcript, record real tokens |
| `src/api/cost.ts` | Server functions for cost analytics |
| `src/lib/hooks/cost.ts` | React Query hooks for cost data |
| `src/components/dashboard/CostPerTicketChart.tsx` | Bar chart: cost per ticket |
| `src/components/dashboard/CostTrendChart.tsx` | Line chart: daily/weekly cost trend |
| `src/components/dashboard/CostByEpicChart.tsx` | Horizontal bar: cost per epic |
| `src/components/settings/CostModelSettings.tsx` | Settings: manage pricing per model |

---

## Verification

1. `pnpm db:generate` — generates migration from schema changes
2. `pnpm db:migrate` — applies migration
3. `pnpm type-check` — no TypeScript errors
4. `pnpm lint` — no lint errors
5. `pnpm test` — all tests pass
6. Manual verification in browser at `localhost:4242`:
   - Dashboard AI Telemetry tab shows cost charts with real data
   - Settings shows cost model management
7. MCP tool verification:
   - `telemetry record-usage` with manual token counts → appears in dashboard
   - `telemetry get-cost` returns per-ticket/epic breakdown
   - `admin list-cost-models` shows seeded pricing
8. Hook end-to-end:
   - End a Claude Code session → Stop hook fires → `capture-token-usage.sh` parses JSONL → `token_usage` rows appear in DB → dashboard shows cost
